decision.triage_reasoning: The patient presents multiple symptoms indicative of potential thyroid dysfunction (fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, dry skin, hair loss, constipation, muscle weakness), which suggests a chronic condition that requires timely evaluation. Although these symptoms are concerning, they are not immediately life-threatening, warranting a SEMI-URGENT classification for further assessment.
decision.notes: No immediate danger; symptoms suggest possible hypothyroidism or related disorder.
decision.context_for_next: Patient needs to be evaluated for possible thyroid dysfunction and management of related symptoms.
The patient needs to be evaluated for possible thyroid dysfunction, and a thyroid panel will be ordered.

decision.context_for_next: Need to order another test related to thyroid dysfunction due to only having one test completed thus far.

decision.recommended_treatment: Levothyroxine therapy to normalize thyroid hormone levels.
